programmes.show_description: Chinese Walls: Nathaniel Parker and Sharon Small are the rather easy-on-the-eye crimebusting duo in the hit series. Lawyer Emily Proctor is found axed to death in Hyde Park.
I No More Desire A Rose: Frank and Lu are hired to unmask a 'Voice in the Dark' who threatens to destroy Wintermas, and Sebastian faces his first solo case.
The Free Waters: With her son now fully-grown, a single mother pursues her dream and starts a private detective agency at her family restaurant in the coastal town of Whitstable.
Unnatural Causes: At the Great Slaughter Mangold Wurzel Festival, the body of a woman is discovered stuffed in the Aunt Sally. Cause of death: Asphyxiation by sawdust in the lucky dip.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Riot City: Conway mans the barricades in a riot situation, but Glaze could find himself facing the real thing.
New. Head and Heart: A corporate head-hunter collapses at the Mill and Daniel tries to persuade her a change of career would be better for her health.
New. My Cup Runneth Over with Love: Julia helps a florist cope with his wife's diagnosis of Huntington's disease and aids an old lady with Alzheimer's.
Truth Will Out: The hospital holds a charity trolley dash, which helps bring Tricia and Carlos closer together, while Will's career hangs in the balance as the hearing gets under way.
Choose Your Illusion: Ruth's recent manic behaviour is exacerbated by the arrival of a patient with mental health problems, while Kirsty and Warren's wedding anniversary ends in disaster.
Riot City: Conway mans the barricades in a riot situation, but Glaze could find himself facing the real thing.
New. Syed starts to unravel when confronted by Masood - will he agree to his parent's demands? Denise is horrified to discover Chelsea's secret.
New. Christian's actions have devastating consequences for Syed, Amira and their families. Chelsea's happiness is short-lived as Denise and Lucas hit her with some home truths.
The Nelson Touch: TV gold from Sunday nights all through the 1980s. James castrates Lord Hulton's pigs, and then wins the pools! Siegfried helps out with a digestive problem.
Leo is alarmed when Orrin reveals his plan to move to Tarrant, and Jan meets her daughter Lynne in Gibraltar.
The Bearded Lady: The very first episode of the mystery drama with Patricia Routledge. On her 60th birthday, Hetty Wainthropp decides to change her life and becomes a detective.
Keeping Up Appearances: No, not Mrs Bucket - Sharon and Tracey! Tracey is shocked to find that two years in prison have finally taken their toll on Darryl.
Cheering Up Gordon: The old men of Yorkshire re-group for more laughs and larking. The trio take a holiday at the seaside with Compo's nephew, Gordon.
The Kink in Foggy's Niblick: Nobody believes Foggy can play golf, particularly when he mentions that he hasn't done so since 1939. Laughs with the ever-young Yorkshiremen.
Going to Gordon's Wedding: The world's longest-running sitcom. Gordon, Compo's shy nephew, is at last getting married to Josie, and Compo considers having a bath for the wedding.

programmes.show_name: PD James: Death in Holy Orders
programmes.show_description: Murder mystery adapted from the book by P D James. The death count continues to rise as Dalgliesh is thwarted by the shrouds of secrecy allowed by the church authorities.
A Face for Radio: 03/08. A baffling murder mystery for the veteran detectives. The team reopens the case of a popular DJ who died on air after an arson attack at an 80s music station.
In the Blink of an Eye: Another beguiling mystery for our two popular detectives. The shooting of a former war photographer, Peter Rooker, leads Lynley and Havers to a tabloid mogul.
The Bearded Lady: The very first episode of the mystery drama with Patricia Routledge. On her 60th birthday, Hetty Wainthropp decides to change her life and becomes a detective.
Random Acts: A woman hires Pearl to look into a large sum of cash left in her mailbox, the restauranteur-turned-detective finds that Whitstable may have a mysterious benefactor.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Hungry for Love: Comedy from Essex, Chigwell to be precise, as Sharon keeps a close eye on Tracey when she goes on a diet after a hurtful remark from husband Darryl.
Crime and Punishment: Hollis stumbles across some grave goings-on at a crematorium, while Brownlow confronts a ghost from his past.
New. The One: A teenage girl, inspired by her parents' perfect marriage, is convinced she's found 'the one' - until she makes a visit to the surgery.
New. Expectations: Archie and Melody argue over JJ's drug use, and Michelle finds herself in the middle of a surrogacy arrangement between two sisters involved with the same man.
Smoke and Mirrors: Mickie reconsiders her future following criticism from Mubbs, Jess decides to put her own feelings before her father's wishes and Owen reassesses his priorities.
The Lion Roars: Kirsty prepares to leave Warren, and the staff are devastated they failed to notice the extent of Ruth's mental health problems.
Crime and Punishment: Hollis stumbles across some grave goings-on at a crematorium, while Brownlow confronts a ghost from his past.
New. The Masoods are the talk of the Square. To Whitney's horror, Billie is determined to attend his army interview. Meanwhile, Liz returns from Spain.
New. A furious Qadim returns to the Square with a score to settle. Pat and Peggy prepare for their hot dates - with the same man! Becca hatches a plan, but will Fat Boy play ball?
Blood and Water: Timeless fun with the Yorkshire vets. James is caught in the middle of a 50-year war of silence between rabbit breeder Oliver Strong and cabbage grower Roland Strong.
Jack, Bill and Leo face stormy waters as they set sail together, while Sir Edward returns to Tarrant with business on his mind.
Eye Witness: Patricia 'Hyacinth Bucket' Routledge stars in the peculiar mystery series. Wainthropp Detective Agency is up and running, and Hetty must find a missing bird-watcher.
Tinker, Tailor: Comedy from the famed Essex siblings. Sharon begins to wonder if her husband was right when he told her there was a 'grass' planted in his cell.
Isometrics and After: More fun with the three old men of Yorkshire. Compo and Clegg are not as fit as they should be, according to Foggy, so they embark on a fitness regime.
Ferret Come Home: Vintage sitcom with some lovely scenery and the three aging Yorkshire lads. Compo's passion for Nora Batty clashes with his concern for his missing ferret.
Getting on Sidney's Wire: The long-running show with the three old devils of Yorkshire. The trio's attempts to do some handy work around the cafe are doomed to failure.
The Face of the Enemy: The Roman Catholic priest spies trouble when Lady Felicia returns to Kembleford from Africa, having split from her lover Benedict Northam. Mark Williams stars.
Dem Bones: It's the Great Slaughter Giant Marrow Contest, but before the judging can commence, a gruesome discovery is made in Edie's allotment plot... Lots of recent human bones!
Loyalties and Royalties: Those amiable detectives brush the dust off another unsolved mystery. When a guitarist is killed, Gerry gets to meet his heroes, rock band Bad Faith.
